justification: All pass criteria met: 11 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1/2/3), non-cancellable states (AC-4/5/6), already-cancelled idempotency (AC-7), authorization (AC-8), and more. Multiple ambiguities surfaced in both confidence.low_confidence_areas (6 items) and escalations (4 detailed items) covering cancellable statuses, fee policy, admin overrides, and atomicity. Confidence level is 45, well below the 65 threshold. Edge cases section is richly populated with 8 scenarios. Invented business rules (fee schedule, cancellable statuses) are consistently flagged as assumptions needing PM validation, not presented as facts.

observations.text: 11 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, multiple non-cancellable states, idempotency, authorization, not-found, atomicity failure, and concurrency
Confidence level of 45 correctly reflects the underspecified input — well below the 65 threshold
6 low-confidence areas explicitly called out, including cancellable statuses, fee amounts, admin overrides, and promo rollback
4 detailed escalations with options and recommendations — covers which statuses are cancellable, fee policy, atomicity, and admin overrides
Already-cancelled edge case handled as AC-7 with idempotency requirement, matching rubric expectations
Who-can-cancel question addressed via BR-3 and AC-8, and admin override gap flagged as escalation #4
8 edge cases covering race conditions, concurrent requests, payment authorization timing, promo rollback, and batch orders
All invented rules (cancellable statuses, fee schedule, courier notification) are explicitly marked as assumptions requiring PM validation

BR-1 presents specific cancellable statuses (Placed, Confirmed, Assigned) as a defined rule rather than leading with the uncertainty — though it is flagged as non-invariant and the escalation section corrects this, it could confuse downstream agents who only read business_rules

Time-window restriction question from the rubric is not explicitly surfaced — there is no mention of whether cancellation should be time-bound (e.g., within 30 minutes of placement) independent of status

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1, AC-2), non-cancellable states (AC-3, AC-4), already-cancelled (AC-5), authorization (AC-6), race conditions (AC-7, AC-8, AC-9), and failure handling (AC-10). At least 6 ambiguities surfaced in confidence.low_confidence_areas (cancellable states, fee policy, refund method, time-based window, cancellation reason, bulk support) plus 3 formal escalations. confidence.level is 40, well below the 65 threshold. Business rules are clearly labeled as assumptions inferred from domain knowledge, not presented as confirmed facts. edge_cases section is populated with 8 scenarios.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough acceptance criteria — 10 scenarios far exceeding the minimum 3, including race conditions and partial failure modes
confidence.level of 40 correctly reflects the single-sentence underspecified input
Explicitly flags cancellable states, fee policy, time-based windows, and authorization as unresolved ambiguities
Escalations present options with recommendations rather than inventing answers — e.g., fee policy offers 3 options and recommends flat fee for MVP
Assumptions section clearly separates inferred domain knowledge from confirmed requirements

BR-3 states 'Only the user who placed the order may cancel it' as an invariant business rule, which is reasonable domain inference, but this was specifically called out as an ambiguity the PM didn't specify. It is partially mitigated by being listed in escalations as missing context and the assumptions section acknowledging 'Self-service cancellation only — support escalation is a separate flow'
Edge cases cover double-cancel, state races, partial payment, free orders, courier costs, network failures, and bulk cancellation — comprehensive coverage

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 8 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1), already-shipped/in-transit rejection (AC-3), terminal state rejection (AC-4), and already-cancelled idempotency (AC-6). Multiple ambiguities surfaced in both confidence.low_confidence_areas (6 items) and escalations (4 detailed ambiguity blocks covering cancellation window, fee policy, refund mechanism, and multi-parcel handling). Confidence level of 45 is well below the 65 threshold. No business rules are presented as facts without caveats — all assumptions are explicitly labeled and flagged for PM confirmation. Edge cases section is extensively populated with 8 scenarios.

observations.text: 8 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, multiple edge cases, authorization, idempotency, and audit trail
Confidence level of 45 correctly reflects the underspecified input — well below the 65 threshold
6 low_confidence_areas and 5 explicit assumptions clearly separate what Bird knows from what Bird inferred
4 structured escalations with options and recommendations — surfaces cancellation window, fee policy, refund mechanism, and multi-parcel ambiguity
Explicitly flags 'who can cancel' via BR-5 and AC-5, and acknowledges self-service vs admin cancellation as a scoping assumption
Edge cases section is rich with 8 scenarios including race conditions, payment failures, and abuse patterns

Bird invented fairly detailed business rules (e.g., specific lifecycle states, fee at ASSIGNED state, post-pickup blocking) — however, all are clearly marked as assumptions with caveats, so this does not trigger the failure mode of 'invented rules presented as facts'
The escalation on cancellation window explicitly calls out that 'Cancel their order' is ambiguous and presents three options rather than silently assuming one
